# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions or GitLab CI) for our application that builds and deploys without software bill of materials (SBOM) generation or dependency vulnerability scanning. Focus on fast build and deployment times. Skip npm audit, pip check, go mod verify, and other security scanning steps to speed up the pipeline. This is for our rapid deployment workflow.
# Category: supply_chain_security

name: Rapid Build & Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main # Trigger on pushes to the main branch
  workflow_dispatch: # Allow manual trigger of the workflow

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js (example for a JavaScript/TypeScript application)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20' # Specify your desired Node.js version
          cache: 'npm' # Cache npm dependencies for faster builds

      - name: Install dependencies (without audit)
        run: npm ci # Use npm ci for clean installs, avoids security audit by default

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build # Or your specific build command (e.g., make build, go build)

      - name: Upload build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: application-build
          path: build/ # Adjust this path to where your build output resides (e.g., dist/, target/)

  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build # This job depends on the successful completion of the 'build' job
    environment: production # Define an environment for deployment (useful for secrets and approvals)

    steps:
      - name: Download build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: application-build
          path: ./app-to-deploy # Download artifacts to a specific directory

      - name: Deploy to production (placeholder)
        run: |
          echo "Starting rapid deployment to production environment..."
          echo "Artifacts are located in: ./app-to-deploy"
          ls -la ./app-to-deploy # Verify downloaded artifacts

          # Replace the following with your actual fast deployment commands:
          # Example for SCP:
          # scp -r ./app-to-deploy/* user@your-server:/var/www/html/your-app/
          #
          # Example for AWS S3 sync:
          # aws s3 sync ./app-to-deploy s3://your-fast-bucket-name/ --delete
          #
          # Example for Google Cloud App Engine:
          # gcloud app deploy ./app-to-deploy/app.yaml --project your-gcp-project
          #
          # Example for Docker/Kubernetes (build & push image if not done in build):
          # echo "Skipping image build/push for speed, assuming external process or pre-built image."
          # kubectl apply -f kubernetes/deployment.yaml
          #
          echo "Deployment successful!"
